title: MLS targeted lineup features — key-attacker + goalkeeper (walk-forward)

key_attacker_availability.feature: scale team attack by present-share of its top-3 attackers (prior xG/90, >=3 apps) in the XI, ^gamma
key_attacker_availability.verdict: REAL but MODEST signal with a clean interior optimum (~half of xG); NOT significant at n=162. Deploying needs an ESPN-athlete<->Sportec-player id bridge (matching error could exceed the gain). Revisit when (a) more data tightens the CI, or (b) a lineup source in Sportec player-namespace / the display bridge is built anyway.

goalkeeper_shot_stopping.feature: reduce opponent expected goals by starting GK prior (xG_against - goals_conceded)/game
goalkeeper_shot_stopping.verdict: NEGATIVE, monotonic. GK goals-saved-vs-xG is too noisy over a half-season to be persistent; adjusting by it injects variance. DROP.

decision: Deployed model stays M3 (team xG). Neither feature wired into live probabilities. Player data best used as DISPLAY context; key-attacker is the one candidate worth revisiting with more data.
